task_completion.description: Verify the 'Multiparameter Monitor' device adapter is selected and running in OpenICE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an OpenICE device adapter window showing 'Multiparameter Monitor' as the active or started device.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Vital Signs' clinical application is open in the OpenICE Supervisor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenICE Supervisor window with the 'Vital Signs' application actively displaying patient data.

task_completion.description: Verify the heart rate is induced to a tachycardia state (between 150 and 300 BPM).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a heart rate (HR) value between 150 and 300 BPM displayed in the Vital Signs application or the device adapter controls.

task_completion.description: Verify a visual tachycardia alarm is triggered in the Vital Signs application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for visual alarm indicators in the Vital Signs app, such as flashing red colors, warning text, or a highlighted heart rate section indicating an alarm state.

task_completion.description: Verify the alarm state screenshot is saved to the correct Desktop path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'tachycardia_alarm_evidence.png' on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop/) or an image viewer showing this file was saved.

task_completion.description: Verify the simulation verification log is created with the required content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text file named 'simulation_verification_log.txt' on the Desktop containing a recorded peak heart rate value and a description of the visual alarm indicator.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenICE GUI to change the heart rate.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for cursor interactions, clicks, or value changes within the OpenICE device adapter or simulator GUI, rather than terminal commands bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the saved screenshot accurately depicts the OpenICE application in an alarm state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image file 'tachycardia_alarm_evidence.png' should clearly show the OpenICE Vital Signs interface with an active alarm, not a blank screen, generic image, or web search result.

integrity.description: Verify the text report contains specific, observed details rather than generic prompt text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in 'simulation_verification_log.txt' should state a specific numeric heart rate (e.g., '180 BPM') and describe the actual visual alarm seen on screen (e.g., 'flashing red box'), rather than just copy-pasting the task instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using the 'Multiparameter Monitor' device adapter and the 'Vital Signs' clinical application in OpenICE. A valid tachycardia event for this simulation must have a heart rate between 150 and 300 BPM. The screenshot must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/Desktop/tachycardia_alarm_evidence.png' and the text report at '/home/ga/Desktop/simulation_verification_log.txt'.
